market_summary: March 2026 was defined by a sharp risk-off rotation driven by the escalating Middle East conflict (Iran war, Strait of Hormuz closure) and a hawkish Federal Reserve that held rates at 3.50-3.75% while raising its inflation forecast to 2.7%. The S&P 500 fell ~7% from its January all-time high, posting four consecutive weekly losses — the longest losing streak in over a year. Energy was the clear sector winner (+5% MTD) benefiting from supply disruption, while tech (-8%) and financials (-6%) bore the brunt of the selloff. VIX climbed to 26.78, signaling sustained fear. Market internals showed widening dispersion and broadening participation, with small caps and equal-weight indexes outperforming cap-weighted benchmarks.

performance.note: System launched 2026-03-22 — only seed run data, no closed trades yet

Geopolitical events (Middle East) are the primary market driver, not earnings or technicals
Fed removed the rate-cut safety net — 'don't fight the Fed' applies here
Energy outperformance is supply-driven and could reverse sharply on de-escalation
VIX > 25 means reduce size, tighten stops, favor defensives — no heroes
